title: Search heuristics choose non-defensive rook moves under king attack
reference_note: The forced-mate bad rook moves are the proof target; Stockfish's exact best defensive move varies by depth, but b2b1 and b2a2 are consistently refuted by ...Rc5-h5 and ...Qxf3/Qf2 mating lines.

before.baseline: pre-fix baseline commit 3099825, before search changes; correction history, LMP, and null move pruning were enabled by default

fix.summary: Correction history now updates only after root searches, and LMP/null move pruning are guarded in tactical king-pressure positions.

root_cause.summary: Correction history was mutating evaluation during the same recursive search, making sibling positions order-dependent. Late move pruning removed quiet defensive resources in the color-swapped variant. Null move pruning hid the shallow original defensive move. Together they produced optimistic scores for passive rook moves while the king was under a mating attack.
